title: phacelia vallis-mortae var. vallis-mortae
intro: Phacelia vallis-mortae var. vallis-mortae is a plant species in the borage family (Boraginaceae) that is native to the southwestern United States. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and fight off infections. The plant's leaves and flowers have been used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures that have been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory infections, while also promoting overall health and well-being. Additionally, Phacelia vallis-mortae var. vallis-mortae has been used to support digestion, reduce stress, and promote a healthy immune system.
